card.honor: The Honor of your presence is requested

expectations.label: A Guest’s Guide
expectations.heading: The Experience
expectations.subtitle: Essential details for our celebration across two cultures.

items.title: A Royal Union
items.desc: Assamese fire rituals meet European vows. A bilingual ceremony weaving two heritages into one story.

items.title: Festive & Vibrant
items.desc: Indian traditional or Western formal. Jewel tones and gold are encouraged. Please avoid solid white or black.

items.title: The Assamese Feast
items.desc: Slow-cooked duck, mustard fish, and tea-garden specialties. A fusion of local delicacies and global favorites.

items.title: Rhythm of the Bihu
items.desc: Traditional folk drums followed by a global DJ set. Bring your dancing shoes for a night beneath the stars.

items.title: Golden December
items.desc: Warm, sunny days (22°C) followed by crisp, cool nights. A light shawl or jacket is recommended for the evening.

items.title: Atithi Devo Bhava
items.desc: Experience the legendary warmth of Assam. Expect open hearts, endless tea, and a home away from home.

activities.label: While You're Here
activities.heading: Things to Experience
activities.subtitle: Assam rewards the curious traveller. Here are our favourite things to do — experiences we'd love to share with you beyond the wedding itself.
activities.noteQuote: "Jorhat is our home — and we hope it becomes a small part of yours. Take it slowly. Eat well. Watch the river."
activities.footerLabel: We can't wait to explore with you

items.title: Kaziranga at Dawn
items.desc: At sunrise, the mist parts to reveal one-horned rhinos grazing beside the Brahmaputra. Join a jeep or elephant safari through UNESCO-listed Kaziranga — home to more rhinos than anywhere else on earth, alongside tigers, wild elephants, and hundreds of migratory birds.

items.title: Majuli Island
items.desc: The world's largest river island and a cradle of Vaishnavite culture. Explore ancient satras (monasteries), watch mask-making artisans, and cycle through paddy fields as the Brahmaputra wraps around you. Best reached by a short ferry from Jorhat.

items.title: Ahom Heritage Trails
items.desc: Walk among the imposing Rang Ghar pavilion and Kareng Ghar palace in Sibasagar — 600-year-old monuments of the Ahom kingdom that ruled Assam for nearly six centuries without a single Mughal conquest.

items.title: Jorhat Food Trail
items.desc: Follow the aromas through Jorhat's markets: freshly smoked duck with mustard, sticky rice steamed inside bamboo, and the unmistakable tang of khar. Stop at a local home for a full Assamese thali — food here is not just sustenance, it is ceremony.

items.title: Brahmaputra River Cruise
items.desc: Drift along one of Asia's mightiest rivers as golden evening light paints the water amber. Stop at midstream sandbanks where rare Gangetic river dolphins surface beside the boat, and watch fishermen cast their nets as the sky turns pink.

items.title: Dehing Patkai Rainforest
items.desc: Venture east into one of India's last surviving lowland rainforests. Listen for the rare Hoolock gibbon calling through a canopy shared with clouded leopards, hornbills, and slow lorises — a world that feels entirely untouched.

items.title: Muga Silk Weaving
items.desc: Travel to Sualkuchi — Assam's silk village — to watch artisans transform golden Muga cocoons into the world's only naturally gold silk. The soft clatter of heritage handlooms fills every home, and every weaver has a story spanning generations.

items.title: Dibru-Saikhowa Feral Horses
items.desc: One of the rarest sights in Asia: wild horses roaming freely across the floodplains of Dibru-Saikhowa National Park. An early morning jeep ride brings you close to herds that have run untamed since the Indo-Burma border wars.

milestones.title: The Beginning of Us
milestones.desc: It began with a planned dinner in Osnabrück that quickly turned into a spontaneous escape to the Alpine peaks of Oberstdorf—proving that the most beautiful journeys are the ones we never saw coming

milestones.title: Across Borders & Mountains
milestones.desc: A year defined by shared laughter and quiet discoveries, leading us to the simple, profound realization that 'home' is no longer a place, but a person

milestones.title: A Promise by the Water
milestones.desc: Amidst the thunderous mist of the Rheinfall on a sun-drenched afternoon, a simple question was asked, and a lifetime together began with a 'Yes'

milestones.title: Two Worlds, One Heart
milestones.desc: A private promise made under Danish skies; a quiet 'I do' shared in intimacy. Now, we turn East—to the heart of Assam—where two worlds, two cultures, and two families finally celebrate as one.

discoverAssam.heading: A Land of Blue Hills
discoverAssam.intro: Beyond the wedding bells lies a gateway to the mystic East. From the rolling emerald tea estates of Jorhat to the ancient rhythms of the Brahmaputra, we invite you to see the world through our eyes.
discoverAssam.feature1Desc: Muga silk, woven with stories of generations.
discoverAssam.feature2Desc: A dawn safari in Kaziranga, home to the Rhino.
discoverAssam.quote: "Lush green tea gardens, and fresh river breezes. A place where time slows down, and every moment feels like a sip of serenity."
discoverAssam.wish: We truly wish you could be there with us

regrets.heading: We will miss you
regrets.quote: "A celebration is only as bright as the people who share it. Though you cannot be with us in person, we carry your love in our hearts."

venue.label: The Venue
venue.name: The Royal Park
venue.desc: The reception will start from 2PM you can come at any time you like and stay as long as you want. It will last till 10PM. A small ceremony will take place from 8PM and last till 10PM
venue.descMehendi: This is where the bride and everyone who wants a Henna Tattoo will be. There will be a small function with music, food and dance. It will start at 5PM and last till 10PM

travelPortal.label: Your Guide
travelPortal.heading: Travel Concierge
travelPortal.footer: We are here to help you every step of the way.
travelPortal.guidedBooking: Want us to guide you? Send us your email and we'll call you right back with your booking.

flightGuide.heading: The Journey to Assam
flightGuide.step1Desc: Secure your journey to Delhi (DEL) or Kolkata (CCU).
flightGuide.step2Desc: Book your final connection to Jorhat (JRH).
flightGuide.entryDesc: For our German and International guests, an e-Visa is required for entry into India.
flightGuide.entryRecommend: We recommend applying at least 4 weeks before travel.

travelCards.journeyBody: We've curated a complete guide for our international guests—covering flights from Germany & China, local stays, and visa requirements.

assamPrep.label: The Traveller’s Toolkit
assamPrep.heading: Essential Preparation
assamPrep.attireFront: Breathable linens for the golden sun; a soft wrap for the river breeze.
assamPrep.attireNotesDesc:
• Pack: Cotton, Linen, Silk
• Evening: A pashmina or light blazer
• Footwear: Comfortable flats for garden walks
assamPrep.healthFront: Tropical essentials to keep you refreshed in the lush greenery.
assamPrep.healthNotesDesc:
• Shield: High SPF & Insect repellent
• Hydrate: Carry a reusable bottle
• Note: Personal meds are best brought from home
assamPrep.tempoFront: Leave the rush behind. In Assam, we live by the rhythm of 'Lahe Lahe'.
assamPrep.tempoNotesDesc:
• Meaning: 'Slowly, slowly'
• Rule: No tight schedules
• Goal: Disconnect to reconnect with the river
